raw_consumer_statement:
XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX XXXX  XXXX  XXXX  : I received an alert for a transaction of {$3200.00} on my CitiBank Credit Card charged to XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX I immediately canceled the card and disputed the charge. It is very strange because CitiBank almost always requires a second verification for larger transactions. 

XX/XX/XXXX : Called CitiBank to follow up on the dispute. They stated that in XXXX business after the dispute date ( XX/XX/XXXX ) that it would be resolved. 

XX/XX/XXXX : Called CitiBank again, they told me to Called the Dispute team, who then told me to call the XXXX XXXX team. So three calls later, and they stated that they had tried to call me previously ( I have no missed calls from Citi Bank ) for information regarding the disputed charge. 
They took the opportunity to ask me these questions, and then sent an automated email saying that they'd tried to call me for more information. So, I called them back yet again ; they confirmed that they had received my information, so I asked them for a confirmation email that I had spoken with them and given the information needed. They stated that they could not send this and that the dispute would be resolved within 90 days. I do not think they are being reasonable in this situation and simply passing around the hot potato to different teams and not resolving my situation. The charge was {$3200.00}, this is crazy
